title: Income Tax Return Filing in India | Expert ITR Consultant
mainHeading: Income Tax Return Filing Service in India

hero.headline: Fast & Accurate Income Tax Filing with Experts
hero.subtext: Trusted by individuals & businesses. Maximize deductions. Avoid penalties.

introduction.heading: Introduction and Its Compliance
body: Income Tax Returns (ITR) is a must for those who are earning above the limit set by the Income Tax Act, 1961. ITR filing is nothing but informing your income, expenses, exemptions, deductions and taxes paid to the Indian Government Income Tax Department. There are various ITR forms (ITR-1 to ITR-7) depending on the taxpayer's category and type of income. Filing ITR on time and accurately leads to tax law compliance and saves the individual from any legal implications or penalties. Professionals Law offers professional ITR filing services for the salaried, freelancers, businesses, HUFs, firms and companies, which comprises of claiming for maximum deductions, reporting the refunds accurately and adhering with the laws based upon the changing tax trends.

whyNeeded.heading: Why It Is Needed
body: ITR filing is legally mandatory for individuals and businesses crossing the basic income threshold. It is an official record of your income and taxes paid and it's important, you will need it to apply for loans, visas or to get your taxes back. Penalties, interest or prosecution may be incurred in the event of non-compliance. Filling for ITR also assist the government to evaluate your tax liability and to keep a tab on our finances. Professionals Law will prepare your return accurately to reduce the chances of examinations or notices and to maximize your tax savings by evaluating all relevant deductions and exemptions.

body.title: Avoid Penalties and Legal Action
body.description: Timely ITR filing helps you stay compliant with income tax laws and avoid penalties, interest or even prosecution for tax evasion or non-disclosure.

body.title: Facilitates Easy Loan and Visa Approval
body.description: Banks and embassies require ITR records for processing personal, home, business loans or visa applications. Filed returns improve financial credibility.

body.title: Claim Tax Refunds
body.description: If excess TDS has been deducted, ITR filing is the only way to claim legitimate refunds from the Income Tax Department.

body.title: Carry Forward Losses
body.description: Filing ITR before the due date allows you to carry forward business or capital losses for future tax adjustments.

body.title: Acts as Proof of Income
body.description: ITRs serve as official proof of income, particularly for self-employed individuals or freelancers who don’t receive Form 16.

categories.applicantType: You are required to file ITR if:
criteria: Your total annual income exceeds ₹2.5 lakh (individuals below 60), ₹3 lakh (senior citizens) or ₹5 lakh (super senior citizens).
You want to claim an income tax refund.
You are a company or firm, regardless of income.
You have foreign income or own foreign assets.
You have deposited over ₹1 crore in a bank account, spent over ₹2 lakh on foreign travel or paid electricity bills exceeding ₹1 lakh.
You are carrying forward a loss to the next financial year.

documents: Salary slips or business income details

body.description: Professionals Law collects your income and deduction details.
Submit required documents (Form 16, bank statements, etc.).
Professionals Law determines the applicable ITR form based on your income type.
Income, deductions, taxes paid and TDS are compiled.
Summary shared with client for final approval.
Return filed on the Income Tax Portal using DSC or EVC.
ITR-V acknowledgment generated and sent to the client.

body.title: Late Filing Fee
body.description: Up to ₹5,000 under Section 234F (₹1,000 if income is below ₹5 lakh).

body.title: Interest on Tax Due
body.description: 1% per month under Section 234A.

body.title: Prosecution
body.description: For willful non-filing or evasion, imprisonment of 3 months to 7 years may apply.

body.title: Loss of Deductions
body.description: Certain deductions and loss carry-forward benefits are not available if ITR is filed late.

body.title: For Individuals & HUFs (non-audit)
body.description: 31st July of the assessment year

body.title: For Companies or Audited Accounts
body.description: 31st October of the assessment year

body.title: For Transfer Pricing Cases
body.description: 30th November

body.title: Belated or Revised Return
body.description: 31st December of the assessment year

body.question: Who is required to file ITR?
body.answer: Any individual or entity earning income above the basic exemption limit must file ITR.

body.question: Which ITR form should I use?
body.answer: The applicable form (ITR-1 to ITR-7) depends on your income source, type of entity and exemptions claimed.

body.question: Can I file ITR myself or do I need professional help?
body.answer: While self-filing is possible, professional services like Professionals Law’s ensure error-free filing and maximum tax benefit.

body.question: What happens if I miss the deadline?
body.answer: You may file a belated return with a late fee. However, you lose some benefits like carrying forward losses.

body.question: Can I revise my ITR after filing?
body.answer: Yes, revised returns can be filed until 31st December of the assessment year.

body.question: Is Aadhaar mandatory for filing ITR?
body.answer: Yes, Aadhaar is required for filing and verifying your return.

body.question: How long does it take to get a refund?
body.answer: Typically, 15 to 45 days, but it depends on the processing speed of the Income Tax Department.

body.question: What if my income is below the taxable limit?
body.answer: You can file a NIL return, which is helpful for future loans, visas or income proof.

body.question: Does Professionals Law help with notices or scrutiny after filing?
body.answer: Yes, Professionals Law provides post-filing support and responds to notices on your behalf.
